title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After hearing a muffled sigh, Anthony climbs through a locked window into a dim room. He discovers a pale, emaciated foreigner with a bandaged head, whose hands and feet are tied. As Anthony approaches, Mr. Hiram Fisk appears at the door, holding a large blue automatic pistol and commanding Anthony to raise his hands, noting they were on the same train at Victoria Station.

On Tuesday evening, the Earl of Carringham, Virginia, and Bondel discuss Anthony’s sudden departure in the study. Lord Carringham expresses his unease regarding the lack of security and the disappearance of Fischer, who was last seen in the rose garden. Tredwell interrupts to announce that the French detective, Monsieur Lemont, has arrived.

Lemont informs the group that he has made a significant discovery regarding a suspicious guest who arrived from a remote location via South Africa. Virginia challenges Lemont, arguing that Victor von Winkle (Mr. Fuchs) is more suspicious than the missing Mr. Kede, citing his secretive behavior and presence near the scene of the murder. Lemont counters by revealing that Mr. Fuchs is actually a private detective for Pinkerton who came to the house to follow Victor Wang. 

The discussion turns to the pursuit of a target, with Lemont expressing determination to catch him. Virginia defends Anthony Kede, swearing he did not kill Prince Michael. Lemont suggests an alternative possibility: that Boris the Hessian killed the Prince out of revenge for past wrongs.
